message:
feat(scripts): add snapshot gate and golden update scripts, wire into CI

Two new scripts for the deterministic runtime_math golden snapshot pipeline:

- snapshot_gate.sh: CI regression gate that regenerates kernel snapshot
  fixtures via 'harness snapshot-kernel' (seed=0xDEAD_BEEF, steps=512,
  mode=both) and verifies sha256 against committed golden checksums in
  tests/runtime_math/golden/sha256sums.txt. On mismatch, emits a unified
  diff (first 200 lines) to stderr and exits non-zero. This catches any
  unintended drift in RuntimeMathKernel deterministic behavior.

- update_golden_snapshots.sh: Intentional update path for when kernel
  snapshot behavior changes with an isomorphism proof. Regenerates the
  golden fixture and recomputes sha256sums.txt in-place, then self-
  verifies. Parameters (seed, steps) match snapshot_gate.sh exactly.

- ci.sh: snapshot gate step added between 'cargo test' and perf gate,
  ensuring every CI run validates snapshot determinism.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -25,6 +25,16 @@ cargo test --all-targets
 echo "PASS"
 echo ""
 
+echo "--- module inventory drift check ---"
+scripts/check_module_inventory.sh
+echo "PASS"
+echo ""
+
+echo "--- snapshot gate (runtime_math golden) ---"
+scripts/snapshot_gate.sh
+echo "PASS"
+echo ""
+
 echo "--- perf gate (runtime_math + membrane) ---"
 scripts/perf_gate.sh
 echo "PASS"

@@ -0,0 +1,53 @@
+#!/usr/bin/env bash
+# Golden snapshot regression gate for runtime_math telemetry.
+#
+# Generates deterministic kernel snapshots via glibc-rs-harness and verifies
+# sha256 against the committed golden set under tests/runtime_math/golden/.
+set -euo pipefail
+
+ROOT="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")/.." && pwd)"
+GOLDEN_DIR="${ROOT}/tests/runtime_math/golden"
+OUT_DIR="${ROOT}/target/runtime_math_golden"
+
+GOLDEN_SHA_FILE="${GOLDEN_DIR}/sha256sums.txt"
+GOLDEN_SNAPSHOT="${GOLDEN_DIR}/kernel_snapshot_smoke.v1.json"
+
+OUT_SNAPSHOT="${OUT_DIR}/kernel_snapshot_smoke.v1.json"
+
+if [[ ! -f "${GOLDEN_SHA_FILE}" ]]; then
+  echo "snapshot_gate: missing golden sha file: ${GOLDEN_SHA_FILE}" >&2
+  exit 2
+fi
+if [[ ! -f "${GOLDEN_SNAPSHOT}" ]]; then
+  echo "snapshot_gate: missing golden snapshot: ${GOLDEN_SNAPSHOT}" >&2
+  exit 2
+fi
+
+mkdir -p "${OUT_DIR}"
+
+echo "=== snapshot_gate ==="
+echo "golden_dir=${GOLDEN_DIR}"
+echo "out_dir=${OUT_DIR}"
+
+# Keep parameters in sync with tests/runtime_math/golden/kernel_snapshot_smoke.v1.json.
+cargo run -q -p glibc-rs-harness --bin harness -- snapshot-kernel \
+  --output "${OUT_SNAPSHOT}" \
+  --mode both \
+  --seed 0xDEAD_BEEF \
+  --steps 512
+
+set +e
+(cd "${OUT_DIR}" && sha256sum -c "${GOLDEN_SHA_FILE}")
+rc=$?
+set -e
+
+if [[ "${rc}" -ne 0 ]]; then
+  echo ""
+  echo "snapshot_gate: sha256 mismatch; diff vs golden (first 200 lines):" >&2
+  diff -u "${GOLDEN_SNAPSHOT}" "${OUT_SNAPSHOT}" | head -n 200 >&2 || true
+  exit 1
+fi
+
+echo ""
+echo "snapshot_gate: PASS"
+

@@ -0,0 +1,34 @@
+#!/usr/bin/env bash
+# Regenerate committed golden runtime_math snapshot fixtures + sha256sums.
+#
+# This is the intentional-update path when kernel snapshot behavior changes
+# and you have an isomorphism proof for the change.
+set -euo pipefail
+
+ROOT="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")/.." && pwd)"
+GOLDEN_DIR="${ROOT}/tests/runtime_math/golden"
+
+SNAPSHOT_FILE="${GOLDEN_DIR}/kernel_snapshot_smoke.v1.json"
+SHA_FILE="${GOLDEN_DIR}/sha256sums.txt"
+
+mkdir -p "${GOLDEN_DIR}"
+
+echo "=== update_golden_snapshots ==="
+echo "golden_dir=${GOLDEN_DIR}"
+
+# Parameters define the canonical golden scenario.
+cargo run -q -p glibc-rs-harness --bin harness -- snapshot-kernel \
+  --output "${SNAPSHOT_FILE}" \
+  --mode both \
+  --seed 0xDEAD_BEEF \
+  --steps 512
+
+sha256sum "${SNAPSHOT_FILE}" | awk '{print $1"  kernel_snapshot_smoke.v1.json"}' > "${SHA_FILE}"
+
+(cd "${GOLDEN_DIR}" && sha256sum -c "${SHA_FILE}")
+
+echo ""
+echo "update_golden_snapshots: updated:"
+echo "- ${SNAPSHOT_FILE}"
+echo "- ${SHA_FILE}"
+
